item.title: Ios Keychain
item.description: In this article I’m trying to study how keychain works. Metaphor There once lived a monkey 🐒 George.

item.title: Ios Decrypting Applications Overview
item.description: There are several tools available that perform decryption of iOS applications. iOS applications that are downloaded from AppStore are encrypted.

item.title: Ios Intercepting Traffic
item.description: There might be several reasons to intercept traffic: just sniffing or also modifying it. In this article I’m going to describe basic techniques of intercepting traffic coming on and from iOS devices.

item.title: Android Cheatsheet
item.description: INFO TO GET Answer package_name path to apk Get package name: # option 1 frida-ps -U | grep [partial_name] # option 2 drozer console connect run app.

item.title: Android Cheatsheet
item.description: Information ANSWER iDevice UDID Package name App’s folder with bin (UUID) App’s folder with data (UUID) Get iDevice UDID # option 1 ioreg -p IOUSB -l | grep "USB Serial" # option 2 idevice_id -l # get UDID with ideviceinstaller # option 3 system_profiler SPUSBDataType | sed -n -e '/iPad/,/Serial/p;/iPhone/,/Serial/p;/iPod/,/Serial/p' | grep "Serial Number:" # # option 4 instruments -s devices Get package name: # option 1 frida-ps -U | grep [partial_name] ipainstaller -l | grep [partial_name] Get folder with bin and with data (UUID) # option 1 ipainstaller -i [package_name] # option 2 objetion -g [package_name] explore > env

item.description: # oh my zsh bash themes sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)" # sh -c "$(fetch -o - https://raw.

item.title: Ios Loggs
item.description: About iOS logs here. Syslog According to Apple docs, NSLog now doesn’t write to syslog, therefore I didn’t find a syslog.

item.description: Install Download MacTex here and install. Install SoX. Then, the following [steps](pip install –pre scapy[complete]) are tested on MacOSX:

item.title: Plan
item.description: Intro Physical drives: HDDs Flash memory RAM - Udemy SDF course Memory Layout (for an application) How it works File Systems FAT Apple NTFS HFS+ Data recovery Cases Research itself: personal insights, techniques and strategies

item.title: Mach O File
item.description: Intro Using otool we can get general info about the Mach-O file: otool -h [app_name] > Mach header magic cputype cpusubtype caps filetype ncmds sizeofcmds flags 0xfeedfacf 16777228 0 0x00 2 115 11384 0x00218085 Quick refernce on the meaning:

item.title: Dshell
item.description: Installing Install with Python3. sudo python3 -m pip install [tar_file] # option 1 pip install dshell # option 2.

item.title: BTFM
item.description: Dshell # MacOSX path to directory with pyenv (example): /Users/[user_name]/.pyenv/versions/3.8.5/envs/python3/lib/python3.8/site-packages/dshell/ decode -l # list decoders decode -d [decoder_name] # decoder info # who talked to whom decode -d ip [capture_file_name].

item.description: Intro At the very beginning of my career I found it hard to grasp the notion of memory layout and stack.

item.title: Laboratory Setup
item.description: This article is a walkthrough of setting up the lab for pentesting iOS applications and forensic analysis of iDevices and Android devices (the main purpose of a tool, installation steps and possible issues along the way).

item.title: How I Met Radare2
item.description: Wednesday 7:00 What a nice morning! And it’s so great to be woken up by a high-pitched cry in the ear… Well, after giving my daughter her breakfast and doing all the neccessary hygine procedures for us both, I left her by my side on the development carpet, so that I could develop myself…

item.title: BTFM for Reverse Engineering
item.description: Rabin2 rabin2 -I [progname] # main info about the prog rabin2 -z [progname] # the strings from the binary's data section rabin2 -zz [progname] # all strings from the binary rabin2 -zqq [progname] # strings only without additional info R2 r2 [progname] # launch radare2 > s main # seak main function and go to it > aa # find all strings > v # psedu GUI mode (Hex editor) > VV # graph mode > afvn user_input input # rename local var and all the places it was referenced # rename function > s function_old_name > afn function_new_name > c # show cursor in visual mode Graph mode VV # open Graph # go to a function via shortcut (;[ao]) o # first type 'o' key > a # then enter the second letter from the brakets Test this https://r2wiki.

item.title: Ios Booting Process
item.description: When an iDevice is started, two separate booting processes are taking place: booting of Application Processor and booting of Secure Enclave.

item.title: Jailbreaking
item.description: Types of jailbreaking Tethered jailbreaks don’t persist through reboots, so re-applying jailbreaks requires the device to be connected (tethered) to a computer during every reboot.

item.title: BTFM and RTFM iOS
item.description: This is a combination of BTFM and RTFM (Blue Team Field Manual and Red Team Field Manual) for iOS devices and applications.

item.title: Intro
item.description: UID - key that is fused into Application processor. No one know it and can get from the device.

item.title: Ipa Structure
item.description: When installed on the device, *.ipa is unzipped into a corresponding folder. That’s the contents:

item.title: Apple Devices Encryption
item.description: Intro All this resembles the sad story of a businessman from Eine Woche volle Samstage (1973) by Paul Maar, when a man was so afraid of thieves that he hid the office key in a sock, the sock - in a boot, the boot in a wardrobe locked with a key, the key to the wardrobe hid in his table’s drawer, and the key from the last had lost and by doing so arranged an unscheduled day-off for his employees.

item.title: Encryption
item.description: The main idea behind the scenes is that applications or even OS itself doesn’t know the keys.

item.title: Forensics vs Pentest
item.description: Forensics vs Penetration testing These two fields are closely related, for you have to acquire the same knowledge but you are just using it differently.

item.title: Attacking Android
item.description: Attacking device For this attack there are several types of attacker: • MiM, i.e. a sniffer.

item.description: Android Overview I’m not going to describe in all the details the basics of this architecture for it’s described pretty good here: Mobile Security Guide link.

item.title: How I Met Fragment Injection
item.description: Friday Moscow has again become Mordor-like, the Sun is starting to make its heat more official rather than genuine because it’s getting desperately cold.

item.title: IPC Attacks
item.description: Fragment Injection To read: https://securityintelligence.com/new-vulnerability-android-framework-fragment-injection/ Preamble: Reflection Fragments require Reflection API. What’s that? Compare two ways of instanciating a class:

item.title: How I Met Predator
item.description: Wednesday *Ничего так, тепло.* Где-то в тёмном-тёмном лесу, среди высоких старых деревьев, притаившись в кустах выжидают свою жертву хищники.

item.title: Undesirable Mail
item.description: Thursday, Warm and cloudy. “Open me! I am a payment order! I am important, I swear!

item.title: How I Met .NET Confuser
item.description: Tuesday, Прохладно, как будто вот-вот пойдет дождь, но нет…. Приятно бывает встретиться лицом к лицу с сильным противником, потому что именно в такие моменты, после таких “битв”, становишься сильнее.

item.title: How I Met Hugo
item.description: There was a time when I was desperately trying to become a web programmer (those wonderful days of naïve curiosity🤦‍♀️).

item.title: About me
item.description: The quieter you become, the more you can hear… (c) The story I want to tell… … or how I entered the InfoSec field

item.title: Achievements
item.description: Penetration tester at SolidLab Ltd In May, 2019 I’ve taken a new career path to widen my knowledge and experience in information security as a penetration tester at SolidLab.

item.title: Contact
